channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: When Does Diablo 4 Season 12 End? Diablo Season 13 Lord of Hatred Expansion Start Date & Times, Explained
item.content:encoded:
As we move through 2026, it’s nearly time for another major expansion in Diablo 4, and that’s the Lord of Hatred update that brings Season 12 to an end, and kickstarts Season 13 in style. Check out Lord of Hatred’s start date and times.

Diablo 4 Season 13 Lord of Hatred is the natural successor to 2025’s Vessel of Hatred. There’s a lot of anger in Diablo 4, isn’t there? Season 12’s Season Slaughter has been a good one, but it’s time to see what hell awaits in Blizzard’s next Diablo 4 expansion.

Diablo 4 Season 12 End Date

Diablo 4 Season 12 Season Slaughter ends on April 28, meaning you haven’t got long left until the next one begins.

Season 12 introduced a wealth of new gameplay features: The Killstreak System, Bloodied Sigils, as well as the insanely cool touch where you get to play as The Butcher!

  • More Insider Gaming: Diablo 4 Gets Major Skill Tree Updates & More in Upcoming DLC

As with all seasonal updates, the content and novelty grow stale, and luckily for you and Blizzard, something cool is up next—Season 13 and the Lord of Hatred expansion for 2026.

When Does Diablo 4 Season 13 Lord of Hatred Start? Dates & Times, Explained

Diablo 4’s Season 13 ‘Lord of Hatred’ content is expected to go live on April 28, and it should have a start time of 10 AM PT.

It’s hard to believe we’re at Season 13 already, but this is a special one because it introduces Lord of Hatred. It brings with it the Paladin Class, the Warlock, and much more.

  • More Insider Gaming: Diablo 4’s Chinese Version Has Been Censored in a Weird Way

For a more accurate release time closer to your region or time zone, check out this table, complete with dates and times.

Which aspect of Lord of Hatred has got you the most excited? Has it got the potential to top Vessel of Hatred? Let us know through the Insider Gaming Discord, and share what you think about the state of Diablo 4 right now.

For more content, read about Crimson Desert devs revealing upcoming features and content, and a Metal Gear Solid movie adaptation being officially confirmed.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

item.title: Best Pokémon Champions Singles Teams: Singles Tier List
item.content:encoded:
Pokémon Champions may not have a complete Pokédex to work with, but there are still a huge number of Pokémon to consider recruiting, and some stand among the best options for Singles Teams.

Pokémon Champions has two types of approach to battles, with Singles battles seeing players select three Pokémon to face off in 1v1 battles, while Doubles, used in VGC competitively, has two Pokémon on the field from both sides at a time.

The best Pokémon for Singles and Doubles can vary, so Insider Gaming has taken a look at the best Pokémon for Singles battles that you should consider.

Pokémon Champions Singles Tier List

Hippowdon and Garchomp are the best Pokémon to use in Singles battles in Pokémon Champions, both of which are definitely worth utilizing.

Hippowdon is an extremely tanky Pokémon capable of constantly chipping away at opponents’ health with the Sand Stream ability, while the fact that it’s pure Ground type gives it better Type matchups. Additionally, moves like Stealth Rock, Yawn, and Slack Off work extremely well with a stall strategy.

Meanwhile, Garchomp is among the best Physical attackers in Pokémon Champions, with a decent Speed stat to boost, and the Rough Skin ability is a good counter to physical attacks. With a wide variety of moves and well-rounded base stats, Garchomp can be crafted into many forms, making it difficult to predict, and it has an accessible Mega Evolution.

  • More Insider Gaming: Best Pokémon Champions Incineroar Build

Other Pokémon to consider in Singles competition include Meowscarada, Archaludon, Hydreigon, and Greninja.

In the tier list below, we’ve outlined the best Pokémon to use in head-to-head battles, but remember, they don’t necessarily apply to doubles battles.

What Makes a Good Singles Pokémon in Pokémon Champions?

Pokémon used in Singles competition in Pokémon Champions must possess the ability to stall opponents, deal significant damage, or tank attacks while utilising set-up moves, or, ideally, a combination of several.

Set-up moves like Stealth Rock are great early moves to get consistent damage on the field, while Abilities like Hippowdon’s Sand Stream are also decent at dealing regular damage every turn. When paired with healing moves or moves that deal status effects, these can be extremely frustrating for opponents.

  • More Insider Gaming: Best Pokémon Champions Replica Team Codes

Alternatively, having a strong Pokémon that can sweep a wide variety of opponents is also beneficial, particularly those who have few type weaknesses and various attacks. The downside, however, is if this Pokémon is eliminated, you might fall behind if you don’t have others ready to step up in your party.

Ultimately, though, it’s all about how you utilize the Pokémon at your disposal, and boasting one of the best Pokémon for Singles battles doesn’t guarantee success, as the choices you make, Movesets, Held Items, and overall build are extremely important.

What Pokémon are you using most in Singles battles in Pokémon Champions? Share your teams in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Pokémon Champions, check out leaks of the next Battle Pass, and take a look at the Speed Tiers.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Is Windrose On PlayStation, Xbox, Or Nintendo Switch? All Console Platforms, Explained
item.content:encoded:
It’s almost time to set sail across the seven seas, as Windrose is finally entering Early Access after an incredibly successful open beta. It’s got every pirate fan on the edge of their seat, and for good reason, too.

Whether you’re engaging in high-stakes naval battles or building your perfect fort, completing the 70-hour campaign or exploring the numerous islands, there’s tons of content to unpack, as a grand adventure awaits in Windrose.

It’s so exciting that console fans are also desperate for its arrival, but will Windrose Early Access be available on PlayStation, Xbox, or Nintendo? Find out below.

Is Windrose On PlayStation?

Sorry, PlayStation fans, it’s not good news: Windrose will not be releasing on the PS5 alongside its PC launch.

It’s not too surprising, in all honesty. The game is only launching in Early Access, and judging by the developer’s description, it could stay that way for at least two more years.

  • More Insider Gaming: Windrose Early Access Release Date Countdown

However, it doesn’t seem like the kind of game that would be impossible to port onto consoles, with the third-person action-adventure gameplay typical for the platforms. However, until the game is in a complete state, it’d be far too difficult to consistently update on PlayStation 5.

Is Windrose On Xbox?

It’s the same story as PlayStation, as Windrose will not be released on Xbox Series X|S alongside its PC launch.

If it’s not coming to PlayStation, then it usually means that it’s not coming to Xbox, and the same can be said for Windrose. Again, when the game officially releases, the answer could change, but for the time being, it will remain a PC exclusive.

Is Windrose On Nintendo Switch?

This one is perhaps the most unsurprising, but no, Windrose is not going to be releasing on either Nintendo Switch systems anytime soon.

  • More Insider Gaming: Does Windrose Have PvP?

The scale of Windrose would make the content complete version of the game difficult enough to port onto a Nintendo system, but whilst it’s still in Early Access? It’s a definite no. That could always change in the future, of course, but right now, your best bet is to start saving up for a PC, unless you’re ready for a hefty wait.

What platform would you want to play Windrose on? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Insider Gaming platform guides, check out the following:

  • Pokémon Champions
  • Darwin’s Paradox
  • Mouse: PI For Hire

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

item.title: Pokémon Champions Speed Tiers: Fastest Pokémon, Explained
item.content:encoded:
Pokémon Champions is the new face of competitive VGC in the franchise, introducing a host of new players to the competition, and knowing the Speed tiers are vital for success.

While a host of different stats and approaches are valuable information for competing in Pokémon Champions, Speed will often be one of the most important Stats, as having a higher total will result in your Pokémon attacking first in the majority of situations.

If you’re unsure which Pokémon are among the fastest in Pokémon Champions and want more details, Insider Gaming has you covered.

What Are The Fastest Pokémon in Pokémon Champions?

The list of fastest Pokémon in Pokémon Champions is largely dominated by Mega Evolutions, with Mega Alakazam and Mega Aerodactyl sitting at the top of the pile.

Eight of the fastest 10 Pokémon in Pokémon Champions are Mega Evolutions, with the only exceptions being Dragapult, the fastest non-Mega Pokémon in the game, and Jolteon.

  • More Insider Gaming: Best Pokémon Champions Replica Teams

The likes of Talonflame, Weavile, Noivern, and Meowscarada also boast impressive Speed stats that can give an edge in battle, and are certainly worth considering in party composition.

The table below lists the fastest Pokémon available in Pokémon Champions, courtesy of @sbe_u on X, with columns for natures and choice scarf.

How to Make Pokémon Faster in Pokémon Champions

Speed stats of Pokémon in Pokémon Champions can be boosted in a number of ways, though some will never be quick enough to rival those on the list of the fastest Pokémon.

Selecting a Nature that improves the Speed stat of a Pokémon, like Timid, Hasty, Jolly, and Naive, can provide a hefty boost, with the counter being lowering one of the other stats.

Additionally, items like a Choice Scarf are also worth utilizing, which boosts the holder’s Speed stat by 50%. However, the holder is locked into the move selected until it switches out of battle.

  • More Insider Gaming: Best Incineroar Build in Pokémon Champions

While it won’t boost the Speed of a Pokémon overall, using priority moves can push your Pokémon to the front of the queue when attacking, with the likes of Fake Out, Shadow Sneak, and Trick Room given an advantage.

Finally, while Trick Room won’t make your Pokémon faster, it will reverse the turn order for five turns, resulting in slower Pokémon attacking first.

Which Pokémon are you using the most in Pokémon Champions? Share your teams in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Pokémon Champions, find out how to Mega Evolve, and take a look at leaks for the next Battle Pass.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Fnatic Take Down Vitality; Team Heretics Lose on Koshmaras Debut
item.content:encoded:
Fnatic took down Vitality to reassert their VCT EMEA supremacy, winning 2-0 in Week 2 of EMEA Stage 1 groups and moving to a 2-0 record.

Elsewhere, Team Heretics fell to Team Liquid in Martynas ‘Koshmaras’ Namikas’s Tier 1 debut, following the sudden benching of Berkcan ‘ComeBack’ Şentürk.

Boaster gets the best of his former Fnatic teammates

The matchup was highly anticipated, with Europe’s most dominant force in Fnatic taking on their departed star Timofey ‘Chronicle’ Khromov. The two had also met in Kickoff, with Fnatic taking the win, but this time was different.

The match was effectively the first time this year that both teams had met with their full rosters, as Vitality had to wait for their academy graduate Ștefan ‘Sayonara’ Mîtcu to reach his 18th birthday.

That happened in the offseason, allowing for the two rosters to clash on even terms, but again it was Fnatic who got the best of their regional rivals.

Unusually, Jake ‘Boaster’ Howlett became the man of the hour in the overall tight-fought contest. In such matches, it is the clutch fact that so often becomes the determining factor, something Fnatic’s IGL delivered in spades.

Boaster provided high impact on Split, joining Emir ‘Alfajer’ Beder as the highest-rated player on the map in the 13-11 victory, before a memorable Breeze also saw a highlight for the veteran IGL.

The Brit took a 1v2 postplant clutch against his former teammates Nikita ‘Derke’ Sirmitev and Chronicle, outbraining Chronicle with an unexpected stick of the spike.

The back-and-forth affair continued to a 13-10 conclusion in Fnatic’s favour, as the recently re-signed Kajetan ‘kaajak’ Haremski starred in the victory.

Despite losing both maps, Chronicle showed his overall class in the series, delivering a 41/31/15 KDA in the series, top-fragging on the server.

Heretics lose on Koshmaras VCT EMEA debut

For Team Heretics, it was a third consecutive loss in VCT, bridging their final defeat at VCT Kickoff and their Week 1 Stage 1 loss to NAVI, which prompted the immediate roster change.

The extremely sudden change of personnel was described by Neil ‘neilzinho’ Finlay as a “fundamental disagreement after the NAVI game in how we see the game as a team and the fundamentals that have been established in this team from day one.” Adding, “He does not fit in this roster.”

ComeBack agreed with the verdict in his own post regarding the departure, stating, “Our playstyles and approaches to the game were complete opposites, and at this point, parting ways was the right decision for both sides.” (translated)

The result was Koshmaras’ immediate move to Team Heretics. The Lithuanian shares a national link with his new IGL, Ričardas ‘Boo’ Lukaševičius, but more importantly, is understood to be more in line with Heretics’ team needs.

With only a few days of practice, Heretics managed to run Liquid relatively close on their own map pick of Pearl 13-10 before Liquid took their own pick of Bind 13-8.

While some grace will be given to Heretics as they attempt to integrate Koshmaras, fans will not want to see the roster sacrifice the whole season. The roster is still, at its core, the ambitious project that won EWC 2025 and finished runners-up at Champions 2024.

Heretics move to 0-2 in Group A with FUT, Gentle Mates, and Karmine Korp presenting opportunities for the roster to recover in EMEA Stage 1.

Can Heretics turn their season around? Discuss that on our Discord!
For more Valorant news, see the details of VCT’s 2027 revamp.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson
